---
title: "Project3 Data Transformation Part"
author: "Muhammad Suffyan Khan"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(janitor)

# 1. Read raw dataset from GitHub
jobs_raw <- read_csv(
  "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MKudanova/Project3_DataScienceSkills/refs/heads/main/DATA/jobsFromGlassdoor.csv"
)

# 2. Clean column names
jobs_raw <- jobs_raw %>%
  clean_names()

# 3. Add job_id
jobs_raw <- jobs_raw %>%
  mutate(job_id = row_number()) %>%
  relocate(job_id)

# 4. Basic cleaning for jobs table
jobs <- jobs_raw %>%
  mutate(
    company_name = str_squish(company_name),
    job_title = str_squish(job_title),
    location = str_squish(location),
    industry = str_squish(industry),
    sector = str_squish(sector),
    revenue = str_squish(revenue),
    type_of_ownership = str_squish(type_of_ownership),
    job_simp = str_squish(job_simp)
  ) %>%
  distinct()

# 5. Define skill columns already present in dataset
skill_cols <- c("python", "excel", "hadoop", "spark", "aws", "tableau", "big_data")

# 6. Create skills table
skills <- tibble(
  skill_name = skill_cols
) %>%
  mutate(skill_id = row_number()) %>%
  select(skill_id, skill_name)

# 7. Create job_skills table by pivoting wide -> long
job_skills <- jobs %>%
  select(job_id, all_of(skill_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -job_id,
    names_to = "skill_name",
    values_to = "present"
  ) %>%
  filter(present == 1) %>%
  select(-present) %>%
  left_join(skills, by = "skill_name") %>%
  select(job_id, skill_id) %>%
  arrange(job_id, skill_id)

# 8. Final jobs table without wide skill columns + remove extra columns
jobs_table <- jobs %>%
  select(
    -all_of(skill_cols),
    -seniority,
    -headquarters,
    -size,
    -same_state,
    -salary_estimate,
    -job_description,
    -revenue
  )

# 10. Write transformed CSVs
write_csv(jobs_table, "jobs_updated.csv")
write_csv(skills, "skills.csv")
write_csv(job_skills, "job_skills.csv")

##Ignore Skills.csv and job_skills.csv created files as result of this code as Madina updated those data files. 

# 11. Quick checks
cat("Jobs rows:", nrow(jobs_table), "\n")
cat("Skills rows:", nrow(skills), "\n")
cat("Job_Skills rows:", nrow(job_skills), "\n")

head(jobs_table)
head(skills)
head(job_skills)
```

This will give the 3 normalized files:

-   `jobs_updated.csv`
-   `skills.csv`
-   `job_skills.csv`
